segments.text: God continued speaking to Job.
Are you still going to fight with the Almighty and try to set him straight? Anyone who argues with God must give some answers.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Job answered the Lord,
Me—I am nothing at all. I have no answers. I put my hand in front of my mouth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have already said far too much and I won't say anything more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then the Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind,
Prepare yourself, be strong, for I am going to question you, and you must answer me.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Are you really going to say my decisions are wrong? Are you going to condemn me so you can be right?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are you as powerful as I am? Does your voice thunder like mine?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why don't you dress yourself with majesty and dignity, and clothe yourself with glory and splendor!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Let loose your fierce anger. Humble the proud with a glance.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Bring down the proud with your gaze; tread the wicked underfoot right where they are.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Bury them in the dust; lock them away in the grave.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then I will also agree that your own strength can save you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Consider Behemoth,  a creature I made just like I made you. It eats grass like cattle.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look at its powerful loins, the muscles of its belly.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It bends its tail like a cedar; its thigh sinews are strong.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Its bones are like bronze tubes; its limbs like iron rods.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It is the most important example of what God can do; only the one who made it can approach it with a sword.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The hills produce food for it, and all the wild animals play there.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It lies under the lotus;  it hides in the reeds of the marsh.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The lotus covers it with shade; the willow trees of the valley surround it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even if the river is in flood, it is not concerned; it remains calm when the Jordan river surges against it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: No one can catch it while it is watching, or pierce its nose with a noose.
segments.speaker: Unknown
